howItWorks.title: Three simple steps to find your new best friend
howItWorks.step1Desc: Explore warm profiles with personality notes and helpful adoption details.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Choose pets that feel right for your home and lifestyle with confidence.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Connect with the rescue team, schedule a visit, and welcome your new friend.

trust.title: Trusted support for every adoption story.
trust.feature1Desc: Your adoption gives a pet a loving home and creates a lasting bond.
trust.feature2Desc: We highlight caring partners and gentle introductions for every match.
trust.feature3Desc: Simple steps, clear guidance, and thoughtful support for your pet journey.

about.intro: We believe every pet deserves a loving home — and every adopter deserves a warm, clear, and simple path to get there.
about.story1: PawMatch was created from a personal journey and a deep love for animals. I'm a student, and I built this website during my degree after realizing how difficult and confusing the adoption process can be.
about.story2: During the war, I adopted my dog Bruno, the brown dog in the photo. Bruno was found wandering in northern Israel, without a home and without anyone knowing where he belonged. When I met him, I understood how many animals are waiting in shelters and rescue organizations, hoping someone will notice them and give them a chance.
about.story3: The adoption itself was one of the most meaningful decisions I have made, but the process was also difficult, unclear, and sometimes overwhelming. I felt that people who want to adopt should not have to struggle with scattered information, complicated forms, and confusing communication.
about.story4: That experience became the reason behind PawMatch. I wanted to create a platform that makes pet adoption warmer, simpler, and more accessible both for adopters and for the animals waiting for a home.
about.story5: PawMatch is more than a student project. It is a small mission to help more pets find loving families and to make the adoption journey easier, clearer, and more human.
about.contactBody: Have a question, suggestion, or want to help more pets find a home? We'd love to hear from you.
about.contactSuccess: Thank you! We received your message and will get back to you soon.
about.placeholderMessage: Tell us how we can help or share your adoption story...

resources.pageSubtitle: Guides, tips, and answers to help you and your new pet have the best start together.
resources.tipsSubtitle: Everything you need to know to give your new pet the best start
resources.faqSubtitle: Answers to the most common questions about adopting through PawMatch
resources.tip1Desc: Pet-proof your space before bringing them home. Secure loose cables, remove toxic plants, and set up a cozy corner just for them.
resources.tip2Desc: Feed age-appropriate food recommended by your vet. Puppies, kittens, adults, and seniors all have different nutritional needs.
resources.tip3Desc: Schedule a check-up within the first week of adoption. Your vet will review vaccines, run a health check, and answer any concerns.
resources.tip4Desc: Daily activity keeps pets healthy and happy. Dogs need walks; cats need interactive play. Even 15 minutes a day makes a big difference.
resources.tip5Desc: Give your pet time to adjust to their new environment. Most animals need 2–4 weeks to settle in — go at their pace, not yours.
resources.tip6Desc: Regular brushing and bathing keeps your pet comfortable and healthy. Long-haired breeds may need daily brushing to prevent matting.
resources.faq1Q: How long does the adoption process take?
resources.faq1A: The process typically takes 3–7 business days from submitting your application. This includes a review of your adoption request, a brief phone interview, and coordination with the shelter. Some organizations may complete it faster.
resources.faq2Q: What documents do I need to adopt?
resources.faq2A: You will need a valid government-issued ID and proof of address. Some shelters may also ask for a rental agreement (to confirm pets are allowed) or a reference from a vet if you have owned pets before.
resources.faq3Q: Can I return a pet if it doesn't work out?
resources.faq3A: Yes. Responsible adoption organizations always accept returns — no questions asked. The pet's wellbeing comes first. Contact the shelter directly and they will guide you through the process.
resources.faq4A: Fees vary by organization and typically range from ₪150 to ₪500. The fee usually covers vaccinations, spaying/neutering, microchipping, and a health check. It is a small fraction of what these services actually cost.
resources.faq5Q: Can I adopt if I live in an apartment?
resources.faq5A: Absolutely. Many cats and small-to-medium dogs thrive in apartments. The key is matching the pet's energy level to your lifestyle and ensuring you can commit to regular walks and enrichment activities.
resources.faq6Q: What if I have other pets at home?
resources.faq6A: Let the shelter know during your application. Many pets have been tested with other animals, and the staff can recommend a good match. A supervised introduction at home is always recommended for the first meeting.
resources.faq7Q: How do I know which pet is right for me?
resources.faq7A: Take our matching quiz! It factors in your home type, lifestyle, energy level, and preferences to calculate a match percentage for every pet. You can also browse profiles and filter by region to find pets nearby.
resources.faq8Q: What support does PawMatch offer after adoption?
resources.faq8A: PawMatch stays in touch after the adoption. You can reach us through the Contact page for advice, questions, or if you need help settling in your new pet. We also share tips and updates through our community.

footer.description: Connecting kind people with warm, friendly pets through an easy and caring adoption experience.
